title: Courier

rationales.taskAutomation: Same-day and specialty courier runs require physical pickup, handling, and delivery across arbitrary addresses; sidewalk-robot and drone pilots cover only a narrow slice of this today.
rationales.toolMaturity: Sidewalk delivery robots and drones are shipped products in specific metro pilots, not a general courier replacement.
rationales.adoption: A handful of markets run robot/drone courier pilots; nearly all courier volume nationally still moves by human driver, biker, or walker.
rationales.agenticExposure: Route sequencing and delivery-status tracking are agent-ownable; the physical pickup-and-handoff core is not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard document/parcel drop-off in accessible locations
tasksGrowing: Customer service recovery for failed handoffs
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-5021, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Robot and drone courier pilots exist in a handful of metro markets but cover a narrow slice of courier volume; the specialized, time-sensitive document runs (medical, legal) that make up much of this role's value are not close to automatable.
